sections.content: The block moved from (28, 26) to (26, 28) and it's now adjacent to the blob's left side. Looking at the image, I can see the magenta block is now touching the blue blob. Let me check if it changed color to purple.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The block at (26, 28) is still magenta, not purple. But looking at the image, it appears to be adjacent to the blob. Previously, blocks at (26, 28) and (32, 34) became purple. But now the block at (26, 28) is still magenta. Maybe the blob changed or the adjacency rules are different.
Wait, looking at the image more carefully, the magenta block at (26, 28) appears to be touching the blob. But it's still magenta. Maybe the color change doesn't happen immediately, or maybe I need to click on the block itself to "place" it?
Actually, looking at the previous history, when blocks were at (26, 28) and (32, 34), they were purple. But now the block at (26, 28) is still magenta. This is strange.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the blocks need to be clicked ON (not moved to) to place them. Or maybe the game state is different now because we recovered from a game over.
Let me try clicking on the block at (26, 28) to see if it changes color.
Actually wait - looking at the image again, I see the magenta block is to the LEFT of the blob, and there's another magenta block below the blob. Let me check if the block at (26, 28) is actually touching the blob or just near it.
Looking at the blob's boundary, the blob is centered at (27, 33) with 69 pixels. The block at (26, 28) is 4 pixels (2x2). The blob's left edge might be around column 30 or so, so the block at (26, 28) might not actually be touching the blob yet.
Let me move the block closer. I'll try clicking at (26, 30) to move it right toward the blob.

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move (26, 28) closer to blob: click (26, 30)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 26, 'col': 30}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
